name: context-management-context-restore
description: Use when working with context management context restore
capabilities: Provides semantic vector search strategies for context retrieval
Implements relevance filtering and ranking for context components
Offers context rehydration patterns with token budget management
Supports session state reconstruction for multi-agent workflows
Includes context merging and conflict resolution strategies
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill is a documentation/guide file containing no executable code. Static findings for 'external_commands' and 'weak cryptographic algorithm' are false positives - the flagged lines contain markdown documentation, code examples in fenced blocks, and YAML frontmatter, not actual shell execution or cryptographic implementations.
valueStatement: This skill helps AI assistants recover and reconstruct project context across complex workflows, enabling seamless continuation of long-running tasks without losing critical information.
userTitle: Restore AI Context with Semantic Memory
limitations: Requires external vector database or storage for context persistence
Does not execute actual context restoration - provides guidance only
Token budget limits apply based on the AI model context window

useCases.targetUser: Developers working on extended AI-assisted projects
useCases.title: Resume complex AI projects
useCases.description: When returning to a long-running AI project after interruption, use this skill to reconstruct the full context including decisions, architecture, and current state.

useCases.targetUser: Teams managing multiple related AI projects
useCases.title: Cross-project knowledge transfer
useCases.description: Transfer semantic knowledge and context between related projects, maintaining consistency and preserving architectural decisions.

useCases.title: Multi-agent workflow coordination
useCases.description: Enable multiple AI agents to share and restore context, maintaining coherent decision trails across complex collaborative workflows.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Context Restoration
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to restore context for project [project-name]. The context is stored at [storage-location]. Please guide me through the restoration process using full restoration mode.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new session with an existing project

promptTemplates.title: Incremental Context Update
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me implement incremental context restoration for my project. I want to update only the changed components since my last session.
promptTemplates.scenario: Updating project context with recent changes

promptTemplates.title: Semantic Context Query
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to retrieve specific context about [topic] from my project. Show me how to use semantic vector search to find the most relevant information.
promptTemplates.scenario: Finding specific information within project context

promptTemplates.title: Context Validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Guide me through validating restored context against the current codebase. I want to ensure consistency and integrity before continuing work.

bestPractices: Always validate restored context against the current codebase before continuing work
Use semantic similarity thresholds (0.75+) to filter irrelevant context components
Implement incremental updates instead of full restoration when possible to save tokens
antiPatterns: Restoring full context without relevance filtering - leads to token overflow
Skipping context validation - may reintroduce outdated or inconsistent information
Ignoring token budget limits - causes context truncation and information loss

faq.question: What is context restoration in Claude Code?
faq.answer: Context restoration is the process of recovering and reconstructing project knowledge, decisions, and state from previous AI assistant sessions, enabling seamless workflow continuation.

faq.question: Do I need a vector database?
faq.answer: A vector database is recommended for semantic context retrieval, but you can also use file-system based storage depending on your project complexity.

faq.question: What token budget should I use?
faq.answer: Default is 8192 tokens, but adjust based on your model context window. Leave room for current task execution beyond restored context.

faq.question: How does semantic similarity work?
faq.answer: Semantic similarity uses embedding models to convert context into vectors, then measures cosine similarity between vectors to find conceptually related content.

faq.question: Can this skill execute context restoration?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides guidance, best practices, and implementation patterns. Actual execution requires integrating with your specific infrastructure.

faq.question: What is incremental context restoration?
faq.answer: Incremental restoration updates only changed components since the last session, rather than loading all context. This saves tokens and improves efficiency.
